source.title: Temu and Shein US Package Volume - ShipMatrix Data

extractions.claim: Temu shipped ~1 million packages/day in US during late 2023 holiday season (ShipMatrix)
extractions.evidence: Shein and Temu were each shipping an estimated one million packages a day on average in the U.S., according to parcel-shipping consultant ShipMatrix. The average daily parcel volume in the U.S. was around 82 million that season.

verification.verifier_notes: From ShipMatrix data cited by WSJ and Supply Chain Dive

extractions.claim: Temu US daily volume ~900K packages in July 2024 (ShipMatrix)
extractions.evidence: In July 2024, the e-commerce marketplaces each provided carriers with about 900,000 packages daily in the U.S., according to ShipMatrix data.

verification.verifier_notes: From ShipMatrix data cited by Supply Chain Dive/Fashion Dive

extractions.claim: Post-de-minimis: Temu US daily shipments dropped to 250K-300K packages/day in 2025
extractions.evidence: Temu's marketplace platform ships roughly 250,000-300,000 packages daily in 2025, likely reflecting the impact of de minimis policy changes. The May 2, 2025 exclusion of China and Hong Kong reduced de minimis volumes by 30-35%.

verification.verifier_notes: Lower figure likely reflects post-de-minimis reality. Cross-referenced with 30-35% decline in Section 321 filings.

extractions.claim: US total domestic parcel market: 23.9 billion packages in 2025, $196B revenue (+4.1%), ~91M weekday average
extractions.evidence: The total U.S. domestic parcel market reached 23.9 billion packages in 2025, with revenue of $196 billion (+4.1%), showing low marginal growth. Average volumes were 91 million per weekday.

extractions.claim: ShipMatrix projects US parcel market to grow at 3.9% CAGR to 26.8B packages by 2028
extractions.evidence: ShipMatrix is forecasting that the U.S. parcel market volume will grow at 3.9% CAGR over the next 3 years, reaching 26.8 billion in 2028.
